source.title: ANF06. Fathers of the Third Century: Gregory Thaumaturgus, Dionysius the Great, Julius Africanus, Anatolius, and Minor Writers, Methodius, Arnobius

verses.book: The Extant Fragments of the Five Books of the Chronography of Julius Africanus.
verses.text: Adam, when 230 years old, begets Seth; and after living other 700 years he died, that is, a second death.

verses.book: The Extant Fragments of the Five Books of the Chronography of Julius Africanus.
verses.text: Seth, when 205 years old, begot Enos; from Adam therefore to the birth of Enos there are 435 years in all.

verses.book: The Extant Fragments of the Five Books of the Chronography of Julius Africanus.
verses.text: Enos, when 190 years old, begets Cainan.

verses.book: The Extant Fragments of the Five Books of the Chronography of Julius Africanus.
verses.text: Cainan again, when 170 years old, begets Malaleel;

verses.book: The Extant Fragments of the Five Books of the Chronography of Julius Africanus.
verses.text: And Malaleel, when 165 years old; begets Jared;

verses.book: The Extant Fragments of the Five Books of the Chronography of Julius Africanus.
verses.text: And Jared, when 162 years old, begets Enoch;

verses.book: The Extant Fragments of the Five Books of the Chronography of Julius Africanus.
verses.text: And Enoch, when 165 years old, begets Mathusala; and having pleased God, after a life of other 200 years, he was not found.

verses.book: The Extant Fragments of the Five Books of the Chronography of Julius Africanus.
verses.text: Mathusala, when 187 years old, begot Lamech.

verses.book: The Extant Fragments of the Five Books of the Chronography of Julius Africanus.
verses.text: Lamech, when 188 years old, begets Noe.
